findings.invariant_violated: Every irreversible or expensive operation sits behind a gate and has a way back.
findings.evidence: The customer-purge endpoint hard-deletes accounts synchronously with no confirmation step and no soft-delete window; there is no way to recover a purged account.
findings.why_proof_missed: The endpoint is covered only by a happy-path unit test that asserts a 200, never the irreversibility.
findings.root_correction: Introduce a soft-delete window and a human confirmation gate before hard deletion.
